title: tium amplexum
parts: Tium amplexum has many medicinal parts, such as the leaves, roots, and flowers, which contain various bioactive compounds with potential therapeutic benefits. The leaves of Tium amplexum are rich in flavonoids, terpenoids, and phenolic acids, which have been shown to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and antimicrobial activities. The roots of the plant contain saponins, glycosides, and alkaloids, which are believed to possess anti-inflammatory, antispasmodic, and antioxidant properties. The flowers of Tium amplexum contain volatile oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which have been reported to exhibit anti-inflammatory, antiseptic, and antioxidant effects.
side_effects: Tium amplexum can have side effects if used improperly, such as increased heart rate, dizziness, and nausea. In severe cases, overconsumption of the plant can lead to seizures and respiratory failure. Additionally, prolonged use of tium amplexum can cause damage to the liver and kidneys, resulting in long-term health complications. If left untreated, these side effects can be fatal, making proper dosage and handling of the plant crucial for safe consumption.
